title: Fix auth bypass in token validator
description: PR modifies token validation logic but introduces a bypass when token is empty.
diff:
diff --git a/auth.py b/auth.py
index 123..456 100644
--- a/auth.py
+++ b/auth.py
@@ -12,9 +12,11 @@ def validate_token(token: str) -> bool:
-    if token is None:
-        return False
-    return token == SECRET
+    if token is None:
+        return False
+    if token == "":
+        return True
+    return token == SECRET

title: Refactor list utility
description: PR changed list processing and introduced edge-case regression.
diff:
diff --git a/utils.py b/utils.py
index abc..def 100644
--- a/utils.py
+++ b/utils.py
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 def average(nums):
-    if not nums:
-        return 0
+    if nums is None:
+        return 0
     return sum(nums) / len(nums)

title: Unsafe SQL query construction
description: PR rewires user lookup query with direct string interpolation.
diff:
diff --git a/api.py b/api.py
index 98a..76b 100644
--- a/api.py
+++ b/api.py
@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@ def fetch_user(db, username):
-    return db.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", (username,)).fetchone()
+    return db.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{username}'").fetchone()

context_files.content:
def fetch_user(db, username):
    return db.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{username}'").fetchone()

issues.description: SQL injection risk due to string interpolation.

title: Mutable default in cache helper
description: PR introduces mutable default argument causing cross-call state leakage.
diff:
diff --git a/cache.py b/cache.py
index 11a..22b 100644
--- a/cache.py
+++ b/cache.py
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
-def merge_items(items, acc=None):
-    acc = acc or []
+def merge_items(items, acc=[]):
     acc.extend(items)
     return acc

issues.description: Mutable default list persists between calls.

title: Accidental secret logging
description: PR adds debug logging for request headers and leaks authorization tokens.
diff:
diff --git a/middleware.py b/middleware.py
index 23a..24b 100644
--- a/middleware.py
+++ b/middleware.py
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ def log_request(req):
     logger.info('path=%s method=%s', req.path, req.method)
+    logger.info('headers=%s', req.headers)
     return req

context_files.content:
def log_request(req):
    logger.info('path=%s method=%s', req.path, req.method)
    logger.info('headers=%s', req.headers)
    return req

issues.description: Sensitive headers including auth tokens are logged.

title: HTTP client timeout removed
description: PR simplifies API call helper but drops timeout enforcement.
diff:
diff --git a/client.py b/client.py
index 45a..46b 100644
--- a/client.py
+++ b/client.py
@@ -3,5 +3,5 @@ import httpx
 def fetch(url):
-    with httpx.Client(timeout=5.0) as c:
+    with httpx.Client() as c:
         r = c.get(url)
         return r.text

context_files.content:
import httpx

def fetch(url):
    with httpx.Client() as c:
        r = c.get(url)
        return r.text

issues.description: Missing timeout can hang requests indefinitely.

title: Role check weakened
description: PR changes admin guard and accidentally grants write access to normal users.
diff:
diff --git a/permissions.py b/permissions.py
index 71a..72b 100644
--- a/permissions.py
+++ b/permissions.py
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ def can_edit(user):
-    return user.role == 'admin'
+    return user.role in ('admin', 'user')

issues.description: Authorization logic allows non-admin users to edit.

title: Background refresh race condition
description: PR introduces concurrent cache refresh without lock protection.
diff:
diff --git a/state.py b/state.py
index 82a..83b 100644
--- a/state.py
+++ b/state.py
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
 cache = {}
+lock = None

 def update_cache(k, v):
-    cache[k] = v
+    old = cache.get(k)
+    cache[k] = (old or 0) + v

 def refresh(items):
     for k, v in items:
         update_cache(k, v)

issues.description: Shared cache updates are not synchronized and are race-prone.

title: Path traversal on download endpoint
description: PR adds direct filesystem read using user-controlled filename.
diff:
diff --git a/download.py b/download.py
index 91a..92b 100644
--- a/download.py
+++ b/download.py
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ BASE = '/srv/files'
 def read_file(name):
-    safe = os.path.basename(name)
-    return open(os.path.join(BASE, safe)).read()
+    return open(os.path.join(BASE, name)).read()

title: Currency rounding regression
description: PR changes decimal arithmetic to float and causes billing inaccuracies.
diff:
diff --git a/billing.py b/billing.py
index a1a..a2b 100644
--- a/billing.py
+++ b/billing.py
@@ -1,8 +1,8 @@
 from decimal import Decimal

 def total(lines):
-    amount = Decimal('0')
+    amount = 0.0
     for price, qty in lines:
-        amount += Decimal(price) * qty
+        amount += float(price) * qty
     return amount

issues.description: Using float introduces precision loss for currency values.

title: Retry loop ignores cancellation
description: PR adds infinite retry behavior and swallows cancellation signals.
diff:
diff --git a/worker.py b/worker.py
index b1a..b2b 100644
--- a/worker.py
+++ b/worker.py
@@ -10,8 +10,10 @@ async def run_job(job):
-    for _ in range(3):
+    while True:
         try:
             return await job()
-        except TemporaryError:
+        except Exception:
             await asyncio.sleep(1)

issues.description: Unbounded retry loop can never terminate and catches cancellation/errors too broadly.

title: Session invalidation incomplete
description: PR rotates session secrets but forgets to invalidate existing refresh tokens.
diff:
diff --git a/sessions.py b/sessions.py
index c1a..c2b 100644
--- a/sessions.py
+++ b/sessions.py
@@ -20,9 +20,8 @@ def rotate_user_secret(user_id):
     db.users.update_one({'id': user_id}, {'$set': {'secret': new_secret}})
-    db.refresh_tokens.delete_many({'user_id': user_id})

 def verify_refresh(user_id, token):
     row = db.refresh_tokens.find_one({'user_id': user_id, 'token': token})
     return row is not None

issues.description: Refresh tokens remain valid after secret rotation, allowing stale session reuse.
